text:
Market today: Mood is cautiously mixed. The big story is that Nasdaq-100 gains in H1 2026 were extremely narrow - just 10 stocks carried the index. ADP jobs came in soft (98K vs expectations), which cuts both ways: could mean Fed gets room to ease, but also signals growth is slowing. Your chip/tech names are sensitive to both.

Your holdings: SOXL (-4.31%) is your main concern - it's a 3x leveraged ETF that decays daily, and with margin amplifying any drop, a move toward your $200 stop is a real hit. SPAL (-7.16%) is a brand-new, very volatile leveraged IPO-style ETF already down meaningfully - small size or cut it if conviction is low. PTNM (-15.25%) is deep in the red for a tiny spec with thin data; margin makes this disproportionately painful.

Watch: Whether soft payrolls push rate-cut odds higher (good for QQQ/SMH) or spook growth sentiment (bad). Also watch SOXL's $200 stop - breach would signal semiconductor momentum cracking.

Bottom line: Your leveraged losers are the only real fire to fight today - everything else is holding fine.
plays: SOXL: consider trimming or tightening your stop toward $215-220 given -4.31% loss plus daily decay plus margin - don't let this drift to $200 on autopilot.
SPAL: already -7.16% and it's a fast-moving leveraged name - if you can't articulate a fresh catalyst, cutting small now beats defending a stop at $21.38 on margin.
No new buys today - scanner setups exist (HOOD, CRWD add) but narrow market breadth and soft jobs data don't give a clean fundamental trigger to add leverage here.
